findings.description: The note states that `getStatusCode()` and `getMessage()` are not `@Published` and therefore "г‚ұгѓ—гѓҒг‚±гѓәг‚·гѓ§гѓій–‹з™ғиЂ…гЃЊжӨ­е‹™г‚ігѓәгѓ‰гЃ‹г‚‰е®‰е…ЁгЃ«е‘әгЃіе‡ғгЃ›г‚‹гЃ“гЃЁгЃЊдүқиЁәгЃ•г‚ЊгЃ¦гЃ„г‚‹гЃ®гЃҮ `isSuccess()` гЃ гЃ‘гЃ§гЃ‚г‚‹". The source only shows the `@Published` annotation on `isSuccess()` without any statement about what `@Published` implies for application developers or that the other methods are unsafe to call. This interpretation of `@Published` semantics is not present in this source file.
findings.source_evidence: Source shows `@Published` on `isSuccess()` only, with no explanation of what the annotation means or its implications for non-annotated methods.

findings.description: The hints for s1 are missing `getStatusCode`, `getMessage`, and `TransactionManagementHandler`. `getStatusCode()` and `getMessage()` are method names defined in the `Result` interface, which is the central API of this section. `TransactionManagementHandler` is explicitly referenced twice in s1 (for commit on normal end and rollback on abnormal end).
findings.source_evidence: Source defines `int getStatusCode()` and `String getMessage()` in the Result interface. Source mentions `TransactionManagementHandler` for both commit and rollback behaviors in the handler result tables.
